task: Write a Python function range_summary(nums) taking a list of integers, possibly unsorted with duplicates, and returning a string of the distinct values in ascending order, collapsing consecutive runs into 'start-end' and joining pieces with commas, so [1, 2, 3, 5] becomes '1-3,5'. A single number stays a single number, never a range. An empty list returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function meets all the specified requirements and handles an empty list correctly.
trace: To tackle the task, I first checked for an empty list and returned an empty string immediately. Then, I sorted the unique elements of the input list to ensure they are in ascending order. I used a loop to iterate through the list while keeping track of the start and end of consecutive ranges. When a break in the sequence is detected, I formatted the range appropriately ('start-end' or 'single_number') and added it to the result list. This approach ensures that each distinct number and range is included only once in the output, adhering to the task's requirements.
